mappings.rationale: Goudie and Viles cover geomorphic processes—weathering, erosion, deposition—and the landforms they produce, which is precisely the subject of this section.
Zalasiewicz covers weathering, sediment transport, erosion, and the geological processes that shape the Earth's surface features.
Middleton covers fluvial processes, drainage patterns, river systems, floodplains, deltas, and the fluvial environment described in detail in this section.
Vincent covers the lacustrine environment including lake basins, hydraulics, sedimentation, and the effects of waves and currents discussed in this section.
Middleton covers the desert environment including eolian processes, sand dunes, desert landforms, and desert expansion/contraction described in this section.
Evans covers glacial processes—erosion, transport, deposition—and the landforms they produce including moraines, drumlins, cirques, and fjords detailed in this section.
Woodward covers glacial and periglacial processes, permafrost, and the landforms produced by ice age glaciation described in this section.
Stow covers marine processes including coastal erosion and deposition, submarine canyons, and the continental shelf and slope discussed in this section.
Sheppard covers coral reef formation and coastal features, relevant to the section's treatment of the marine environment and coral reefs.

mappings.vsiAuthor: John A. Matthews, David T. Herbert
mappings.rationale: Matthews and Herbert cover physical geography including geomorphic processes, landform development, and environmental characteristics described in this section.
Redfern provides an overview of surface processes including weathering, erosion, and the formation of major environmental features.
Price covers mountain geomorphology including glacial erosion features, periglacial processes, and the alpine environment discussed in this section.
Ghazoul covers tropical and temperate forest environments, relevant to the section's treatment of the tropical (rain forest) and temperate (woodland) environments.
Furley covers savanna landscapes and geomorphic processes in tropical grasslands, relevant to the section's treatment of the tropical environment.
Covers Arctic geomorphic processes including permafrost, periglacial landforms, and patterned ground discussed in this section's polar environment coverage.
Dodds covers Antarctic glacial processes and polar landforms relevant to the section's discussion of the polar and glacial environments.
McGuire covers volcanic-tectonic processes, landslides, and flooding events that shape Earth's surface features as discussed in this section.
Directly relevant to volcanic-tectonic processes that create and modify surface features, including volcanic landforms discussed in this section.
Ghazoul covers the biological processes that affect rock weathering, soil formation, and sediment yield as described in this section's biosphere interactions.
Molnar discusses the tectonic processes underlying volcanic-tectonic landforms and the concept of uniformitarianism in landform evolution.
Ellis discusses human modification of Earth's surface through land cultivation, mining, and construction, relevant to the section's coverage of humankind's effects on surface features.

Lomolino covers the relationship between landforms and biological distributions, relevant to the section's discussion of biological processes affecting geomorphology and vegetation effects on sediment yield.
Lenton covers the interactions between the lithosphere, hydrosphere, atmosphere, and biosphere that drive the geomorphic processes described in this section.
